1	---
2	ddx:
3	  id: FEAT-005
4	  depends_on:
5	    - helix.prd
6	    - FEAT-007
7	    - FEAT-018
8	---
9	# Feature: Artifact Convention
10	
11	**ID:** FEAT-005
12	**Status:** In Progress
13	**Priority:** P1
14	**Owner:** DDx Team
15	
16	## Overview
17	
18	An **artifact** is any file carrying DDx **identity** — a structured `id` plus
19	optional graph and provenance metadata. Identity may be embedded (in a
20	markdown file's YAML frontmatter under a `ddx:` block) or supplied via a
21	sidecar `<filename>.ddx.yaml` for non-markdown media (diagrams, wireframes,
22	images, prompts, audio, PDFs, binaries, etc.).
23	
24	The `id` field identifies the artifact; the ID prefix conventionally
25	indicates the type (ADR-001, SD-003, MET-007, FEAT-012, etc.). DDx does
26	not hardcode artifact types — it manages the document graph generically.
27	Types are declared by plugins (see FEAT-018, Surface 6); the conventional
28	prefix table below is the fallback when no installed plugin claims a
29	prefix.
30	
31	This replaces the previous approach of dedicated `ddx adr` and `ddx sd` commands. Those are removed in favor of the generic `ddx doc` commands (FEAT-007).
32	
33	## Definition
34	
35	### Authority rule
36	
37	**Identity present → artifact.** A file is an artifact if and only if DDx can
38	resolve identity for it, by either of two mechanisms:
39	
40	1. **Embedded identity** — markdown files with a `ddx:` block in YAML
41	   frontmatter:
42	
43	   ```yaml
44	   ---
45	   ddx:
46	     id: <PREFIX>-<NNN>
47	     depends_on: [<other-ids>]
48	   ---
49	   # Content
50	   ```
51	
52	2. **Sidecar identity** — any file `<path>/<name>.<ext>` accompanied by
53	   `<path>/<name>.<ext>.ddx.yaml` containing the identity block:
54	
55	   ```yaml
56	   # diagram.excalidraw.ddx.yaml
57	   id: DIAG-007
58	   media_type: application/vnd.excalidraw+json
59	   depends_on: [SD-003]
60	   generated_by:
61	     run: <run-id>
62	     prompt: <input-selector>
63	     source_hash: <sha256>
64	   ```
65	
66	There is no second-class "managed asset" concept. Either a file has identity
67	(and is an artifact), or it does not (and DDx ignores it for graph purposes).
68	
69	DDx uses content-based discovery: it scans for embedded `ddx:` frontmatter
70	in markdown and for sidecar `*.ddx.yaml` files within the project's
71	configured roots, subject to an exclusion list of tool-managed and storage
72	directories (e.g. `.git/`, `.ddx/`, `.ddx/plugins/`, `.claude/`, `.agents/`,
73	and any `worktrees/` subtree). Identity comes from frontmatter, not from
74	file paths, but discovery is bounded so plugin-shipped or agent-scratch
75	copies of canonical docs do not collide with the project's own artifacts.
76	
77	### Common Artifact Types (by convention)
78	
79	| Prefix | Type | Typical Location |
80	|--------|------|-----------------|
81	| `FEAT` | Feature specification | `docs/helix/01-frame/features/` |
82	| `ADR` | Architecture Decision Record | `docs/adr/` or `docs/helix/02-design/adr/` |
83	| `SD` | Solution Design | `docs/designs/` or `docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/` |
84	| `TD` | Technical Design | `docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/` |
85	| `TP` | Test Plan | `docs/helix/03-test/` |
86	| `MET` | Metric definition | `docs/metrics/` |
87	| `US` | User Story | `docs/helix/01-frame/user-stories/` |
88	| `DIAG` | Diagram (mermaid, excalidraw, svg) | `docs/diagrams/` |
89	| `WIRE` | Wireframe / mockup | `docs/wireframes/` |
90	| `IMG` | Image asset (hero, illustration) | `docs/images/` |
91	| `PRMT` | Prompt artifact | `docs/prompts/` |
92	| `RSCH` | Research note (IDs `RSCH-NNN`) | `docs/helix/00-discover/research/` |
93	| `REF` | Reference, external source capture (IDs `REF-NNN`) | `docs/helix/00-discover/references/` |
94	
95	These prefixes are conventions — DDx treats them all the same. Workflows
96	(HELIX) may enforce that certain types exist or follow specific templates.
97	
98	Projects may introduce additional conventions such as `AC-*` for acceptance
99	criteria or `TC-*` for test cases. DDx does not privilege those types; it only
100	tracks their IDs and relationships.
101	
102	#### Prefix resolution
103	
104	Prefix lookup is **plugin-defined**. At `ddx doc` time DDx consults the
105	artifact-type index built from installed plugins (per FEAT-018, Surface 6)
106	to resolve a prefix to its governing type, template, and prompts. The
107	conventional prefix table above remains valid as a fallback for prefixes
108	no installed plugin claims, so projects without a workflow plugin still
109	get sensible defaults. Plugin-declared types take precedence when both a
110	plugin entry and a conventional default exist for the same prefix.
111	
112	### Identity Schema
113	
114	Identity carries the same field set whether embedded under a markdown
115	`ddx:` block or stored in a sidecar `.ddx.yaml`.
116	
117	| Field | Type | Required | Description |
118	|-------|------|----------|-------------|
119	| `id` | string | yes | Unique identifier with type prefix (e.g., `ADR-001`) |
120	| `media_type` | string | conditional | IANA-style media type. Required for sidecar identity; inferred from extension for embedded markdown (`text/markdown`). |
121	| `depends_on` | []string | no | IDs of artifacts this one depends on |
122	| `inputs` | []string | no | Input selectors for prompt context resolution |
123	| `generated_by` | object | no | Provenance for generated artifacts: `{ run, prompt, source_hash }`. Records the layer-1/layer-2 run that produced this artifact and the hash of its source inputs. |
124	| `review` | object | no | Staleness tracking (managed by `ddx doc stamp`) |
125	| `prompt` | string | no | Custom prompt for updating this document |
126	| `parking_lot` | bool | no | Skip in staleness checks |
127	
128	Unknown fields are preserved on round-trip.
129	
130	#### Type-specific metadata (outside `ddx:`)
131	
132	Type conventions may carry additional metadata fields at the top level of
133	frontmatter, alongside (not inside) the `ddx:` identity block. The parser
134	treats these as inert pass-through fields. For example, a `REF-NNN`
135	reference artifact captures provenance about an external source:
136	
137	```yaml
138	---
139	ddx:
140	  id: REF-007
141	  depends_on: [RSCH-003]
142	source_url: https://example.com/article
143	source_author: Jane Doe
144	accessed: 2026-05-01
145	---
146	# Reference: ...
147	```
148	
149	Identity (`id`, `depends_on`, etc.) stays under `ddx:`; type-specific fields
150	like `source_url`, `source_author`, and `accessed` live at the top level.
151	
152	#### Type-specific metadata example: MET
153	
154	Metric artifacts (`MET-*`) carry a top-level `metric:` block alongside the
155	`ddx:` identity block. The block is type-specific metadata that the generic
156	parser passes through inert; metric tooling (`ddx metric`, `ddx doc validate`)
157	keys off it.
158	
159	```yaml
160	---
161	ddx:
162	  id: MET-007
163	  depends_on: [FEAT-014, SD-016]
164	metric:
165	  schema_version: 1            # required; v1 is the only accepted value
166	  unit: ms                     # required; free-form (ms, bytes, USD, count, ...)
167	  direction: lower-is-better   # required; lower-is-better | higher-is-better
168	  goal: 250                    # optional; aspirational target (documentary)
169	  budget: 400                  # optional; machine-checkable hard line
170	  source: exec                 # required; v1 enum: exec | external
171	  scope: per-attempt           # required; per-attempt | per-bead | per-feature | global
172	---
173	# Metric: ...
174	```
175	
176	Field semantics:
177	
178	- `schema_version` — pins the metric block schema. v1 is the only accepted
179	  value; future versions extend rather than replace.
180	- `unit` — free-form unit string. Mixed-unit history is refused by
181	  comparison and trend projections (see TD-005).
182	- `direction` — must match the comparison constants used by the metric
183	  runtime (`lower-is-better`, `higher-is-better`).
184	- `goal` vs `budget` — `goal` is aspirational and documentary; `budget`
185	  is a machine-checkable hard line. When a gate's `thresholds.ratchet`
186	  observes this metric, the gate ratchet is authoritative for landing
187	  decisions; `ddx doc validate` emits a warning when `budget` and the
188	  observing ratchet disagree.
189	- `source` — `exec` for metrics emitted by `ddx exec` runs; `external`
190	  for metrics ingested from outside DDx (e.g. cost reports).
191	- `scope` — declares the granularity at which the value is meaningful.
192	
193	`source: derived` is intentionally out of v1; it returns when a real
194	derivation schema exists. `MET-*` artifacts are project-local; multi-node
195	aggregation is deferred to a future MET v2.
196	
197	> **MET v2 backlog (informational):** derived-source metrics with a real
198	> formula schema, multi-node aggregation semantics, structured acceptance-
199	> criteria comparators, and per-MET dashboard wiring are deferred. None of
200	> these are required by v1; they are tracked here so future work can pick
201	> them up without rediscovering the shape.
202	
203	#### `media_type`
204	
205	Identifies how an artifact's bytes should be interpreted. Examples:
206	
207	- `text/markdown` (default for embedded identity)
208	- `image/png`, `image/svg+xml`
209	- `application/vnd.excalidraw+json`
210	- `application/pdf`
211	- `text/mermaid`
212	- `application/vnd.ddx.prompt+yaml`
213	
214	Web UI rendering, regenerate flows, and tooling key off `media_type` rather
215	than file extension.
216	
217	#### `generated_by`
218	
219	Marks an artifact as the output of a DDx run. Carries the run id, the prompt
220	selector, and the `source_hash` used at generation time. Regeneration is
221	source-hash-driven: when inputs change, the source hash diverges and tooling
222	can offer to regenerate. `generated_by` creates a separate provenance edge in
223	the graph (FEAT-007) — it tracks origin, not authority, and does **not**
224	cascade staleness like `depends_on`.
225	
226	## The Artifact Graph
227	
228	All artifacts in a project — markdown and non-markdown alike — form a
229	**single directed acyclic graph** via `depends_on` edges. This graph is the
230	project's authority structure: it encodes which artifacts govern which.
231	
232	```
233	Product Vision
234	  └─ PRD
235	       ├─ FEAT-001
236	       │    ├─ SD-001
237	       │    │    ├─ TD-001
238	       │    │    │    └─ TP-001
239	       │    │    └─ DIAG-001  (excalidraw, sidecar identity)
240	       │    └─ US-001
241	       ├─ FEAT-002
242	       │    └─ ADR-001
243	       └─ MET-001
244	```
245	
246	Every artifact should be reachable from a root (typically the vision or PRD). Orphaned artifacts — those with no incoming or outgoing edges — are a smell that `ddx doc validate` flags.
247	
248	### Graph Properties
249	
250	- **Single graph per project.** All artifacts with identity participate in one graph regardless of media type. There are no separate "ADR graph," "diagram graph," or "feature graph" partitions.
251	- **Edges are explicit.** Only `depends_on` creates authority edges. `generated_by` creates a parallel provenance edge that is not authority. No implicit edges from directory structure or naming.
252	- **Direction is authority.** A `depends_on` edge from B to A means "A governs B" — if A changes, B may be stale.
253	- **Authority staleness cascades.** If a node is stale, all its `depends_on` descendants are transitively stale.
254	- **Provenance staleness does not cascade.** A `generated_by` source-hash mismatch flags only the generated artifact itself, offering regeneration; it does not propagate.
255	- **Types and media are orthogonal.** The graph doesn't care about artifact types or media. An ADR can depend on a feature spec; a metric can depend on a PRD; a wireframe can depend on a solution design. The graph is type- and media-agnostic.
256	
257	## What DDx Does With Artifacts
258	
259	All via `ddx doc` commands (FEAT-007):
260	
261	1. **Discover** — scan for embedded `ddx:` frontmatter in markdown and for sidecar `*.ddx.yaml` files
262	2. **Graph** — build the single project-wide dependency DAG plus provenance edges
263	3. **Stale** — detect when upstream `depends_on` artifacts changed since last stamp; detect generated artifacts whose `source_hash` no longer matches
264	4. **Stamp** — record review hashes after updating a document
265	5. **List** — list artifacts with optional type or media filtering (`ddx doc list --type ADR`, `ddx artifact list --media-type image/png`)
266	6. **Show** — display artifact content/metadata; for non-markdown media, show the sidecar plus a media-typed pointer
267	7. **Validate** — check identity structure, dependency references exist, no circular `depends_on`, no orphans, sidecars match an existing target file
268	
269	## What DDx Does NOT Do
270	
271	- **Scaffold from templates** — agents and workflows create artifacts. DDx doesn't need `create` commands for each type.
272	- **Enforce type-specific structure** — "ADRs must have a Decision section" is a workflow concern. DDx validates the graph; dun validates content structure.
273	- **Hardcode artifact types or media** — no switch statements on type prefixes or media types. The graph treats all artifacts equally.
274	- **Partition the graph** — all artifacts are in one graph regardless of type, media, or directory.
275	- **Execute artifact files directly** — runtime execution belongs to DDx runs (FEAT-010), not the artifact graph itself.
276	- **Recognize a "managed asset" tier** — files without identity are not artifacts, period. There is no halfway state.
277	
278	## Templates and Prompts
279	
280	Artifact types are declared by plugins. The mandated layout (FEAT-018,
281	Surface 6) ships per-type definition directories under each plugin:
282	
283	```
284	<plugin-root>/workflows/**/artifacts/<typeId>/
285	├── meta.yml          # Type metadata, prefix declaration, validation rules
286	├── template.md       # Structural template (frontmatter + sections), or sidecar template for binary types
287	├── prompt.md         # Generation/evolution prompt
288	└── example.md        # Optional worked example
289	```
290	
291	For example, the bundled HELIX plugin ships
292	`workflows/phases/02-design/artifacts/adr/` with:
293	- `meta.yml` — declares `prefix: ADR`, validation rules, dependencies
294	- `template.md` — ADR skeleton with Context/Decision/Alternatives sections
295	- `prompt.md` — prompt instructing an agent how to write a good ADR
296	- `example.md` — a worked example
297	
298	These are **plugin resources**, not CLI features. Agents and workflows
299	read them from the plugin's artifact-type index when operating on
300	artifacts. DDx ships the `ddx` plugin's defaults; other plugins (HELIX,
301	project-local) layer additional or overriding types on top.
302	
303	The doc graph (FEAT-007) can resolve these prompts via input selectors:
304	- `artifact-prompt:ADR:generation` → the generation prompt for ADR artifacts
305	- `artifact-template:ADR` → the template for ADR artifacts
306	
307	### Compat: legacy HELIX shape
308	
309	The pre-FEAT-018 layout is supported as a compat path so existing
310	projects continue to work without migration:
311	
312	```
313	.ddx/library/artifacts/<type>/
314	├── template.md
315	├── create.md          # → indexed as the generation prompt
316	├── evolve.md          # → indexed as the evolution prompt
317	└── check.md           # → indexed as the review prompt
318	```
319	
320	DDx auto-imports legacy entries into the artifact-type index with the
321	type name inferred from the directory. When both a new-shape
322	(`workflows/**/artifacts/<typeId>/`) and a legacy entry exist for the
323	same prefix, the new-shape entry wins. New plugins MUST use the new
324	shape; the legacy shape is frozen — no new types should be added there.
325	
326	## Artifacts and Runs
327	
328	Artifacts stay declarative. They define meaning, authority, provenance, and
329	relationships in the graph. DDx does **not** execute artifact files directly.
330	
331	Runtime invocation belongs to the three-layer run architecture (FEAT-010):
332	`ddx run` (layer-1 single agent invocation), `ddx try <bead>` (layer-2 bead
333	attempt), `ddx work` (layer-3 queue drain). Run records are file-backed and
334	linked to artifacts by ID via `generated_by`.
335	
336	### Boundary
337	
338	- Artifacts declare what matters, who governs it, where it came from, and how
339	  it relates to other artifacts in the graph.
340	- Runs describe one invocation that may produce or consume artifacts.
341	- Run records capture one immutable invocation: raw logs, structured result
342	  data, status, tokens/duration/exit, and provenance.
343	- Runs and run records are not artifacts and do not participate in document
344	  staleness or graph validation.
345	- DDx does not infer run semantics from an artifact type prefix.
346	
347	### Examples
348	
349	- A `MET-*` artifact may link to a layer-1 run definition that emits numeric
350	  observations over time.
351	- A `DIAG-*` excalidraw artifact may carry `generated_by` pointing at the
352	  layer-1 run that produced it; regenerating updates the file and refreshes
353	  the source hash.
354	- Metric comparison and trend views are projections over runs, not evidence
355	  stored in a separate metric-owned runtime backend.
356	- A project may model acceptance criteria or test cases as standalone
357	  artifacts and link them to runs that emit pass/fail or richer structured
358	  results.
359	- A run may evaluate multiple governing artifacts together as long as those
360	  links are explicit in the run record.
361	
362	The shared rule is simple: artifact identity establishes meaning, authority,
363	and provenance; DDx runs establish repeatable runtime behavior and evidence.
364	
365	## Migration
366	
367	The dedicated `ddx adr` and `ddx sd` commands are removed. Their functionality is subsumed by:
368	
369	| Old Command | Replacement |
370	|-------------|-------------|
371	| `ddx adr create` | Agent creates file from template |
372	| `ddx adr list` | `ddx doc list --type ADR` |
373	| `ddx adr show ADR-001` | `ddx doc show ADR-001` |
374	| `ddx adr validate` | `ddx doc validate` |
375	| `ddx sd create` | Agent creates file from template |
376	| `ddx sd list` | `ddx doc list --type SD` |
377	| `ddx sd show SD-001` | `ddx doc show SD-001` |
378	| `ddx sd validate` | `ddx doc validate` |
379	
380	Existing markdown artifacts are unaffected by the multi-media broadening:
381	their embedded `ddx:` identity continues to work and `media_type` defaults
382	to `text/markdown` when absent. Adoption of sidecar identity is opt-in,
383	file by file.
384	
385	## Dependencies
386	
387	- FEAT-007 (Doc Graph) — provides the `ddx doc` commands, sidecar scanner,
388	  `media_type` handling, and `generated_by` provenance edges
389	- FEAT-010 (Runs) — provides the run records that `generated_by` references
390	- Document library with templates
391	
392	## Out of Scope
393	
394	- Type-specific CLI commands (use `ddx doc` generically)
395	- Content structure validation (that's dun/workflow-level)
396	- Template application commands (agents do this)
397	- A "managed asset" or any other second-class artifact tier
398

1	---
2	ddx:
3	  id: TD-005
4	  depends_on:
5	    - FEAT-005
6	    - FEAT-010
7	    - TD-010
8	    - SD-005
9	---
10	# Technical Design: Metric Runtime and History
11	
12	This design extends [TD-010](TD-010-executions.md).
13	Generic execution collection naming, attachment publication, and legacy
14	fallback rules come from the generic execution substrate; this document adds
15	the metric-specific definition fields, run payload shape, and comparison/trend
16	projection rules.
17	
18	## File Layout
19	
20	For the JSONL backend, the logical execution collections map to separate files:
21	
22	```text
23	.ddx/
24	├── exec-definitions.jsonl        # bead-backed execution definition records
25	├── exec-runs.jsonl               # bead-backed execution run records
26	└── exec-runs.d/
27	    └── <run-id>/
28	        ├── result.json
29	        ├── stdout.log
30	        └── stderr.log
31	```
32	
33	Metric-specific inspection is a projection over the generic execution store.
34	No separate `.ddx/metrics/` runtime store is required.
35	
36	Other backends preserve the same logical collections even if they do not map
37	them to JSONL files directly. Their physical storage layout is owned by the
38	backend implementation.
39	
40	## Execution Definition Format
41	
42	Each definition record is a bead-schema row in the `exec-definitions`
43	collection.
44	
45	```json
46	{
47	  "id": "exec-metric-startup-time@1",
48	  "issue_type": "exec_definition",
49	  "status": "open",
50	  "title": "Execution definition for MET-001 startup time",
51	  "labels": ["artifact:MET-001", "executor:command"],
52	  "artifact_ids": ["MET-001"],
53	  "executor": {
54	    "kind": "command",
55	    "command": ["ddx", "bead", "create"],
56	    "cwd": ".",
57	    "env": {
58	      "DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK": "1"
59	    }
60	  },
61	  "result": {
62	    "metric": {
63	      "unit": "ms",
64	      "value_path": "$.duration_ms"
65	    }
66	  },
67	  "evaluation": {
68	    "comparison": "lower-is-better",
69	    "thresholds": {
70	      "warn": 20,
71	      "ratchet": 30
72	    }
73	  },
74	  "active": true,
75	  "created_at": "2026-04-04T15:00:00Z"
76	}
77	```
78	
79	Rules:
80	- The execution definition is metadata, not executable code.
81	- The linked `artifact_ids` set must include exactly one `MET-*` artifact.
82	- `status=open` means the definition is active; `status=closed` means the
83	  definition is retired but preserved.
84	- Only one active definition should exist per metric at a time.
85	- Historical definitions remain readable for comparison and auditing.
86	
87	## Execution Run Format
88	
89	Each execution creates one execution-run bead row in the `exec-runs`
90	collection plus optional sidecar files under `exec-runs.d/<run-id>/`.
91	
92	```json
93	{
94	  "id": "exec-metric-startup-time@2026-04-04T15:01:00Z",
95	  "issue_type": "exec_run",
96	  "status": "closed",
97	  "title": "Execution run for MET-001 startup time",
98	  "labels": ["artifact:MET-001", "executor:command", "kind:metric"],
99	  "run_status": "success",
100	  "definition_id": "exec-metric-startup-time@1",
101	  "artifact_ids": ["MET-001"],
102	  "started_at": "2026-04-04T15:01:00Z",
103	  "finished_at": "2026-04-04T15:01:00Z",
104	  "result": {
105	    "metric": {
106	      "artifact_id": "MET-001",
107	      "definition_id": "exec-metric-startup-time@1",
108	      "observed_at": "2026-04-04T15:01:00Z",
109	      "status": "pass",
110	      "value": 14.6,
111	      "unit": "ms",
112	      "samples": [14.6],
113	      "comparison": {
114	        "baseline": 20,
115	        "delta": -5.4,
116	        "direction": "lower-is-better"
117	      }
118	    }
119	  },
120	  "exit_code": 0,
121	  "attachments": {
122	    "stdout": "exec-runs.d/exec-metric-startup-time@2026-04-04T15:01:00Z/stdout.log",
123	    "stderr": "exec-runs.d/exec-metric-startup-time@2026-04-04T15:01:00Z/stderr.log",
124	    "result": "exec-runs.d/exec-metric-startup-time@2026-04-04T15:01:00Z/result.json"
125	  },
126	  "provenance": {
127	    "git_rev": "abc123",
128	    "ddx_version": "0.1.0"
129	  }
130	}
131	```
132	
133	The execution-run row is the searchable metadata/index record. `result.json`
134	stores the structured metric payload referenced by the row. `stdout.log` and
135	`stderr.log` store raw captured output bodies when present.
136	
137	Migration policy:
138	- New writes target the bead-backed `exec-definitions` and `exec-runs`
139	  collections.
140	- Legacy `.ddx/exec/definitions/` and `.ddx/exec/runs/` data remains readable
141	  as a fallback during migration.
142	- `ddx metric` projections resolve through `ddx exec`; they do not write to a
143	  separate authoritative `.ddx/metrics/` runtime store.
144	
145	The bead-envelope `status` tracks record lifecycle. Domain execution outcome is
146	stored separately as `run_status` to avoid redefining the shared bead field.
147	
148	## Write Mechanism
149	
150	Execution definition and run writes must be safe under concurrent agents.
151	
152	1. Resolve the named bead-backed collection (`exec-definitions` or `exec-runs`).
153	2. Create any large payloads in a temporary attachment directory.
154	3. Acquire the collection lock.
155	4. Publish the attachment directory into its final location.
156	5. Create or update the bead-schema row in the collection using the existing
157	   bead store write path.
158	6. Release the lock.
159	
160	This reuses the bead store semantics for metadata writes while keeping large
161	payloads out of the collection row. A crash may leave an orphan attachment
162	directory, but it must not publish a partial row.
163	
164	## Validation Algorithm
165	
166	`ddx exec validate <definition-id>` performs:
167	
168	1. Resolve the linked `MET-*` artifact.
169	2. Resolve the execution definition by ID.
170	3. Verify the execution-definition bead row is valid for the collection.
171	4. Verify the executor configuration is present and executable.
172	5. Validate metric-specific result and evaluation fields.
173	6. Report actionable errors with the artifact ID, definition ID, and backing
174	   collection that failed.
175	
176	## Run Algorithm
177	
178	`ddx exec run <definition-id>` performs:
179	
180	1. Resolve the execution definition and linked `MET-*` artifact.
181	2. Spawn the command in the configured working directory.
182	3. Capture exit code, stdout, stderr, and elapsed duration.
183	4. Normalize the measured value(s) into `result.metric`.
184	5. Compute comparison output against the configured baseline.
185	6. Write the generic execution-run row and referenced attachments atomically
186	   enough that published rows never point at missing files.
187	
188	## Ratchet Evaluation and execute-bead Landing
189	
190	Metric ratchets feed into `ddx try` merge/preserve decisions.
191	The evaluation model is narrow: results → metrics → ratchet/blocker outcome.
192	
193	**Outcome rules:**
194	- A required execution that fails (non-zero exit or `run_status != "success"`)
195	  blocks the merge landing.
196	- A ratchet regression — where the current run's value exceeds the `ratchet`
197	  threshold in the direction that matters (`lower-is-better` or `higher-is-better`)
198	  relative to the configured threshold — prevents merge. The iteration is
199	  preserved under a hidden ref instead.
200	- Non-required, non-ratcheted metric runs are recorded evidence only; they do
201	  not affect the landing decision.
202	
203	**Threshold fields:**
204	- `thresholds.warn` — emit a warning when this value is exceeded; no blocking
205	- `thresholds.ratchet` — block landing when the current value strictly exceeds
206	  this threshold (`value > ratchet`, not equal-to). For multi-sample runs
207	  (multiple entries in `samples`), use the maximum value for threshold comparison
208	  unless the definition specifies otherwise.
209	
210	Field names are generic scalars, not unit-specific. The `unit` field in the
211	`result.metric` block identifies what the number means.
212	
213	### Acceptance Criteria (ratchet evaluation feeding execute-bead)
214	
215	- Given a metric execution run completes with a value exceeding `thresholds.ratchet` in the blocking direction, when execute-bead evaluates the outcome, then the iteration is classified as not merge-eligible and is preserved under a hidden ref rather than landed.
216	- Given a metric execution run completes with a value between `thresholds.warn` and `thresholds.ratchet`, when execute-bead evaluates the outcome, then a warning is emitted but the iteration remains merge-eligible.
217	- Given a non-required, non-ratcheted metric execution run completes, when execute-bead evaluates the outcome, then the run is stored as evidence and does not affect the merge/preserve decision.
218	
219	## Compare and Trend
220	
221	- Metric compare reads generic execution history, filters by `MET-*` artifact
222	  or definition ID, resolves the requested baseline, and reports a
223	  deterministic delta.
224	- Metric history filters execution-run rows by metric-linked artifact and
225	  prints them in observed order.
226	- Metric trend aggregates the `result.metric` series without rewriting any
227	  stored record.
228	
229	## Collection Ownership and v1 Amendments
230	
231	Metric history is owned by the generic `exec-runs` collection defined in
232	TD-010. There is no separate `metric-runs` collection and no
233	`.ddx/metrics/` runtime store. `ddx metric` is a projection over
234	`exec-runs` filtered by `MET-*` artifact id; metric reads and writes flow
235	through the same execution substrate as every other `ddx exec` consumer.
236	
237	The following v1 amendments apply to the metric projection without
238	changing the collection layout:
239	
240	- **`schema_version`** — the `metric:` frontmatter block on a `MET-*`
241	  artifact (see FEAT-005) carries `schema_version: 1`. Metric tooling
242	  refuses to load a `MET-*` artifact whose `schema_version` is absent or
243	  not equal to the value(s) it understands. Rows already persisted in
244	  `exec-runs` remain readable regardless of the artifact's current
245	  `schema_version`; the version gates artifact interpretation, not
246	  history reads.
247	- **Mixed-unit refusal in compare and trend** — `ddx metric compare` and
248	  `ddx metric trend` refuse to operate when the selected
249	  `result.metric` rows span more than one `unit`, returning an explicit
250	  error. `ddx metric history` continues to surface every row but groups
251	  output by `unit` so older rows under a retired unit remain visible
252	  evidence rather than silently mixed into a current series.
253	- **Reverse-index for `metric show`** — `ddx metric show <MET-id>`
254	  resolves the set of execution definitions whose `result.metric`
255	  observes the named `MET-*` artifact (the gates contributing
256	  observations) by walking the docgraph plus `exec-definitions`. This is
257	  a read-time projection; no new index is persisted.
258	
259	These amendments preserve the bead-backed `exec-definitions` /
260	`exec-runs` collection ownership documented above. No rename to
261	`metric-runs` is implied or planned.
262

---
ddx:
  id: MET-NNN
  depends_on: []
metric:
  schema_version: 1
  unit: <unit>                  # ms | bytes | USD | count | ratio | ...
  direction: lower-is-better    # lower-is-better | higher-is-better
  goal: <number>                # optional aspirational target
  budget: <number>              # optional machine-checkable hard line
  source: exec                  # exec | external
  scope: per-attempt            # per-attempt | per-bead | per-feature | global
---
# Metric: <Short Name>

## What this measures

One paragraph describing the observable, the system boundary, and what a
reader is meant to infer from a single value. Be specific about what is
included and what is excluded.

## Why it matters

Tie the metric back to the governing artifact(s) listed in `ddx.depends_on`.
Explain the decision the metric supports — for example, gating a release,
catching a regression, or guiding a tradeoff.

## How it is observed

Describe the producer of the value:

- **`source: exec`** — name the `ddx exec` definition that emits this
  metric and the field path it populates (e.g. `result.metric.value`).
- **`source: external`** — name the upstream system or report, the
  ingestion pathway, and the cadence.

## Goal vs budget

- **Goal (`<number> <unit>`)** — aspirational target. Documentary only.
- **Budget (`<number> <unit>`)** — hard line. When a gate observes this
  metric via `ddx.execution.metric.metric_id`, the gate's
  `thresholds.ratchet` is authoritative for landing decisions. `ddx doc
  validate` warns when budget and the observing ratchet disagree.

## History and projection

Metric history lives in the generic `exec-runs` collection (see TD-005).
Read it via:

- `ddx metric history MET-NNN` — observed values in order
- `ddx metric show MET-NNN`    — frontmatter, observing gates, partitions
- `ddx metric compare MET-NNN <baseline>` — deterministic delta (refuses
  to mix units)
- `ddx metric trend MET-NNN`   — aggregated series (refuses to mix units)

## Notes

Optional. Capture caveats: known noise sources, sampling assumptions,
unit migrations, or links to follow-on work in the MET v2 backlog.

All four acceptance criteria are already satisfied in the worktree's
base-rev (03eb5607). The amendments and bundle were authored in commit
dc565a65 ("docs(metric): FEAT-005/TD-005 amendments + library/artifacts/met
bundle [ddx-41481396]") which has since been merged into main and is
ancestor of the current checkpoint.

Evidence:

  AC1 — FEAT-005 metric: block schema
    docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-005-artifacts.md lines 152-201
    documents the v1 metric: top-level frontmatter block (schema_version,
    unit, direction, goal, budget, source, scope) as a type-specific
    metadata example parallel to REF.

  AC2 — TD-005 exec-runs collection ownership (no rename to metric-runs)
    docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-005-metric-runtime-history.md
    section "Collection Ownership and v1 Amendments" (lines 230-261)
    explicitly states metric history is owned by the generic exec-runs
    collection and "No rename to metric-runs is implied or planned."

  AC3 — library/artifacts/met/{template,prompt,examples} bundle
    library/artifacts/met/template.md
    library/artifacts/met/prompt.md
    library/artifacts/met/examples.md
    All three files exist.

  AC4 — ddx doc audit clean
    The only integrity issue ddx doc audit reports is a pre-existing
    FEAT-002 -> FEAT-008 -> FEAT-013 -> FEAT-014 -> FEAT-020 -> SD-013
    -> SD-019 -> FEAT-002 cycle that does not touch FEAT-005, TD-005,
    or any MET-* artifact, and predates this bead. It is out of scope
    here.
